prompt_text: Open source or affordable AI tooling tools instead of Haystack
response:
# Open Source & Affordable AI Tooling Alternatives to Haystack

## **Core RAG/LLM Frameworks**

### **LangChain** (Free, Open Source)
- Most popular alternative
- Extensive integrations with LLMs, vector stores, tools
- Strong community support
- Good documentation
- https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain

### **LlamaIndex** (Free, Open Source)
- Specialized for indexing and retrieval
- Better for document-heavy applications
- Simpler API than LangChain
- https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index

### **Semantic Kernel** (Free, Open Source)
- Microsoft's framework
- Language-agnostic (.NET, Python, Java)
- Good enterprise integration
- https://github.com/microsoft/semantic-kernel

## **Vector Databases (Free/Affordable)**

| Tool | Cost | Best For |
|------|------|----------|
| **Chroma** | Free | Lightweight, embedded, prototyping |
| **Weaviate** | Free tier | Open source, scalable |
| **Milvus** | Free | High performance, self-hosted |
| **Qdrant** | Free tier | Fast, Rust-based |
| **Pinecone** | Freemium | Managed (if budget available) |

## **Local LLM Options (Free)**

- **Ollama** - Run LLMs locally easily
- **LM Studio** - GUI for local models
- **GPT4All** - CPU-friendly models
- **Llama.cpp** - Optimized inference

## **Recommended Stack (Minimal Cost)**

```
LangChain/LlamaIndex
    ↓
Local LLM (Ollama/Llama2)
    ↓
Chroma or Milvus (vector DB)
    ↓
Open source embeddings (Sentence-Transformers)
```

**Total cost: $0 for development**

Would you like specific setup instructions for any of these?
